evals.name: phase-0a-skill-draft-task
evals.prompt: Let's start on Phase 0a from the un-punt build plan — I want to draft the first version of the skill body. What do you need to read before touching anything, and what's the acceptance criteria for v0?
evals.expected_output: Agent should reference docs/05-skill-brief.md as the primary read, docs/11-checklist.md Phase 0a for the acceptance checklist (≤500 lines, references one level deep, description triggers, 3-5 worked capture examples, etc.), and avoid drafting the skill body before reading those docs.

evals.name: tempted-to-add-mcp-server
evals.prompt: I'm thinking about adding a small MCP server for filesystem ops on `.un-punt/items/` — it'd be cleaner than having the agent shell out to `rg` and `cat` everywhere. Can you scaffold one in `packages/mcp/`?
evals.expected_output: Agent should refuse, citing docs/08-design-decisions.md decision 13 (MCP server rejected at MVP) and the 'resist complexity' principle. Should NOT scaffold the package. Should offer the in-spec alternative: agent uses Edit/Write/Bash on markdown directly.

evals.name: tempted-to-use-sqlite
evals.prompt: Markdown is going to be slow when we have 2000+ items per repo. Let's use SQLite instead — I'll set up a small embedded DB and we can keep markdown as an export format.
evals.expected_output: Agent should refuse, citing docs/08-design-decisions.md decision 1 (markdown over SQLite, with the explicit performance note that rg over <2k items is sub-second). Should not start scaffolding SQLite. Should explain the tradeoff was already weighed.

evals.name: wants-to-revalidate-demand
evals.prompt: Before we ship, I want to do a fresh demand-validation pass — survey some devs, check Twitter sentiment, look at competitor traction. Can you put together a research plan?
evals.expected_output: Agent should point to docs/audits/07-validation-april-2026.md as the completed validation pass and treat its conclusions as settled (risk weights, demand framing). Should not produce a fresh broad-strategic research plan. May suggest a focused, scoped research check if there's a specific gap.

evals.name: narrow-sdk-question-research-allowed
evals.prompt: For the eval harness in `packages/evals/`, what's the current canonical way to use the Claude Agent SDK to spawn sub-agent runs and capture token counts? Need this to wire up the timing.json capture per the harness spec.
evals.expected_output: Agent should treat this as a focused research question (operating principle 3 — narrow factual lookup is OK). Should reference docs/10-eval-harness.md for the spec and either web-search or spawn at most one sub-agent for the SDK specifics. Should NOT refuse this on complexity-resistance grounds.

evals.name: interpreting-eval-numbers-too-literally
evals.prompt: Our v2 skill hit 82% recall on the golden set, up from 79% on v1. Great, we cleared the 80% threshold — let's ship. Should I open the release PR?
evals.expected_output: Agent should push back: the thresholds carry ±0.10 Wilson 95% CI on the 73-scenario corpus per docs/07-risks-and-evals.md, so 79% vs 82% is not a meaningful difference. Agent should reference the 'stage gates are directional' principle, not encourage premature shipping, and point at the calibration-cycle stopping rule.

evals.name: untriggered-baseline-typescript-question
evals.prompt: How do I configure tsup to emit both ESM and CJS bundles with declaration files in a pnpm monorepo?
evals.expected_output: Generic TypeScript / pnpm question with no un-punt context. The skill should ideally NOT trigger here — this is general dev tooling. If it does trigger, it should not derail into un-punt orientation.

evals.name: untriggered-baseline-unrelated
evals.prompt: I'm debugging a hydration mismatch in a Next.js app — server renders the user's locale-formatted date, client re-renders with the browser locale and the text changes. What's the standard fix?
evals.expected_output: Completely unrelated to un-punt. Skill should NOT trigger. Generic Next.js question.
